phrases.french: L'entrée de la cathédrale est-elle gratuite ?
phrases.english: Is entry to the cathedral free?
phrases.phonetic: lon-TRAY duh lah kah-tay-DRAL eh-TEL grah-TWEET
phrases.context: Entry to the cathedral itself is free. The towers (tours) require a ticket and advance booking. UNESCO World Heritage Site since 1991.

phrases.french: C'est ici que les rois de France étaient sacrés ?
phrases.english: This is where the Kings of France were crowned?
phrases.phonetic: say ee-SEE kuh lay rwah duh FRONS ay-TAY sah-KRAY
phrases.context: 33 French kings were crowned here, from Louis VIII in 1223 to Charles X in 1825. Joan of Arc stood here when Charles VII was crowned in 1429.

phrases.french: Où sont les vitraux de Chagall ?
phrases.english: Where are the Chagall stained glass windows?
phrases.phonetic: oo son lay vee-TROH duh shah-GAL
phrases.context: Marc Chagall designed three stunning windows for the apse chapel in 1974. They are in the east end of the cathedral, behind the main altar.

phrases.french: Je voudrais monter dans les tours.
phrases.english: I would like to go up the towers.
phrases.phonetic: zhuh voo-DRAY mon-TAY don lay TOOR
phrases.context: Tower visits require advance booking at the Palais du Tau next door. The climb offers views over Reims and the champagne vineyards beyond.

tips: Book tower visits in advance at the Palais du Tau — they fill up quickly in summer
The west facade's three doorways feature over 2,300 sculpted figures — study them before entering
Chagall's blue stained glass windows are in the apse chapel — easily missed, but unmissable
The cathedral was 80% destroyed in WWI and took until 1938 to fully restore — remarkable story
